{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks for an analysis to detect tooth extraction in a class II division 1 malocclusion of a 14-year-old boy. Bolton's analysis is commonly used to assess the size and proportion of teeth in relation to each other and the jaws, making it suitable for determining if tooth extraction is necessary. The other analyses mentioned (Pont's, Peck & Peck, and Ashley & Howe's) are not as frequently used for this purpose.", "answer_choice": "A"}
